title: Interface security between operators

compliance.assurance_rationale: BSI-KritisV §8a — UC provides continuous Splunk telemetry evidence for this regulatory requirement, uplifted from contributing to partial after review confirmed the SPL captures relevant monitoring signals.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that BSI-KritisV §8a (Security in IT systems) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.28.9: Interface security between operators.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.28.9' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=network sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h tag=kritis_interface
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes by src_zone, dest_zone, app
| where src_zone!=dest_zone AND app="unknown-tcp"
| sort - bytes
description: Demonstrates alignment with BSI-KritisV expectations for interface security between operators using auditable Splunk evidence.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for BSI-KritisV compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes network and sourcetypes pan:traffic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain `de_kritis_assets.csv` with sector classification and KRITIS identifiers. (2) Link ITSI services to KRITIS objects for business impact overlays. (3) Export monthly control posture snapshots for BSI engagement. (4) Tune thresholds with enterprise risk management and OT security owners. Indexes required: `index=network`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`.
visualization: Matrix (host x module), Bar chart (severity), Table (controls). Key fields: `app`, `bytes`, `dest_zone`, `src_zone`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Grundschutz checks during quarterly reviews, controlled exception cycles, or scheduled refreshes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `de_kritis_assets.csv` with sector classification and KRITIS identifiers. (2) Link ITSI services to KRITIS objects for business impact overlays. (3) Export monthly control posture snapshots for BSI engagement. (4) Tune thresholds with enterprise risk management and OT security owners.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h tag=kritis_interface
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes by src_zone, dest_zone, app
| where src_zone!=dest_zone AND app="unknown-tcp"
| sort - bytes
```

Understanding this SPL

**Interface security between operators (BSI-KritisV)** — Demonstrates alignment with BSI-KritisV expectations for interface security between operators using auditable Splunk evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype="pan:traffic", time bounds, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_zone, dest_zone, app** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where src_zone!=dest_zone AND app="unknown-tcp"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Interface security between operators (BSI-KritisV)** — Demonstrates alignment with BSI-KritisV expectations for interface security between operators using auditable Splunk evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the BSI compliance report and the same reporting window the security team used on paper. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Matrix (host x module), Bar chart (severity), Table (controls).
grandmaExplanation: We show alignment with BSI-KritisV expectations for interface security between operators using auditable Splunk evidence. That matches the critical-infrastructure and reporting work we are asked to do in the region we operate in.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Network_Traffic data model (Network_Traffic.All_Traffic).
